Delicious Filipino BBQ skewers! The meat was well-marinated and tender. A must-try! They are located outside of one of the entrances at Parklea Markets, but you can easily locate them if you just follow the smokey bbq smell in the air. Also, get a halo halo (Filipino dessert) next to their shop called Mix Mix. It's great to support local businesses.

Poor service, no signs up to advise what they’ve run out of and this was just before midday. No chicken skewers, only pork which was fine but 1 hour wait. Could see others frustrated after queuing to be told what they could put up on signs of what’s not available. Steamed buns took 30 mins but not told this before ordering. Had to return one as it was cold in the middle and all they did was microwave it without any ownership. Customer service could definitely be better and managing stock before the lunch time rush or at least better communication. Not worth the wait.

Good value and tasty bbq.
Food was not at optimal temp perhaps prorated slightly earlier ahead of lunch crowd.

name: Johnnie's Doonside
address: 257 Doonside Cres, Doonside NSW 2767, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Drove past Jonnie’s a couple of times and saw they had burger steak so I had to give it a go and I was not disappointed…
I ordered the Burger steak, chicken Inasal and the kalamansi iced tea.
Everything was eaten within 20mins it was that good.

Burger steak - I haven’t had a good burger steak in a while and iv had many and Jonnie’s does it the best. Burger steak cooked perfectly and the gravy is the I’ve tasted in a long time all of that paired with the garlic rice very delicious. (highly recommend)

Chicken Inasal - chicken cooked perfectly juicy and tender, paired with the garlic rice and the sauce was also very delicious
(Highly recommend)

Kalamansi iced tea - very refreshing not so watery not to sweet it was made just right and is now my new favourite drink
(Highly recommend)

Pearl was also very lovely, welcoming and fun to talk to. Place was clean and the atmosphere was great, I would’ve eaten there if it wasn’t so close to closing time.
Food was also very affordable with a huge menu. I will be returning again very soon to try some more food.

This is one of those places that hits all the marks — flavor, service, and experience.

We had the tapsilog, tocilog, spaghetti, and calamansi juice, and everything was spot on. The food was flavorful and comforting, just like home. The spaghetti especially reminded us of Jollibee-style sweetness done perfectly.

Johnnie was so personable and friendly, you can tell he genuinely cares about every guest and every plate that comes out of the kitchen. It’s the kind of place that makes you feel welcomed the moment you walk in.

Definitely a gem in Western Sydney. Can’t wait to come back!

We just arrived back from a holiday in the Philippines and was craving inasal chicken, spaghetti with fried chicken and came across this place - definitely did not disappoint! Very flavourful, cravings satisfied! 😊 Will definitely be back!

Found my new favourite place to dine.
Food is good and customer service is amazing. Reasonable pricing and quick service. 👌🥰 Would recommend 💯

We enjoyed our visit here. Owner was very nice and puts a lot of love in what he does. I enjoyed  The works beef burger and the jojos was decent. Crispy and freshly cooked fried chicken but maybe needing a little more seasoning. Took some time for the food to come out but I didn't mind it since we weren't in a rush. I Kalamansi juice was a little too sweet for me but still good. I'd come back and try one of the silogs next time.

name: Johnnie's Windsor
address: Unit 1/70 George St, Windsor NSW 2756, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: loved the inasal and garlic rice
Great food and service! Can't wait to go back and try more offerings

This place is great. It’s nice to see a different style cuisine in the area. The owner John was such a nice bloke. Had a nice chat with him. I used to eat from a lot of the restaurants/takeaways growing up in the area that have all closed down. It’s good to see a new place with heart. I recommend popping in for a bite and a chat.

Tried beef pares, palabok and chicken inasal. Chicken was so moist and tender but the baste could be more punchy. Palabok did not have the seafood hit I expected. Beef was so tender but gravy was a bit too sweet for me. It needs a bit more ginger and black pepper. But service is 12 out of 10. He catered for everything we requested.
Great taste! I miss eating pares. The pares here is really good! We'll be back!

name: Kapamilya Bakeshop & Takeaway Food
address: 1 Milgate Ln, Campbelltown NSW 2560, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Great little Filipino bakery and food spot.
Tucked away around the corner but within easy access with plenty of parking.
Plenty of parking and friendly staff
The fresh baked cakes are awesome and great value. We enjoyed our banana turon and ube treats.
Customer Service was amazing. They explained all their dishes and we got to try some. We loved the flavours and the different dishes they serve. Lovely local Filipino baked goods and hot food!!
Breads are next level. Snacks ready to go in Empanadas, Siopao, Turon, and Lumpia. Dishes on go at the hot bar from Adobo, Humba, Laing, and Langka. Accessible and authentic Filipino food
Incredible food and amazing service! The woman serving us let us have tasters and everything tasted amazing. You have to try the fried banana with jackfruit sauce!

Amazing foods of the Philippines culture LOVE IT 😊 Typical friendly Filipino service and a clean shop. I RARELY leave reviews but the Kapamilya Bakeshop & Takeaway was a nice exotic treat and I recommend you try their exotic flavours 🍀

name: Kipling's Garage Bar
address: 2 Eastern Rd, Turramurra NSW 2074, Australia
description: Lively, industrial-chic bar in a former garage, serving inventive tapas plates and craft beer.
reviews: So happy with our choice for an anniversary dinner. The atmosphere was cozy and welcoming from the start. The share plates at truly reasonable prices was such a rare and amazing experience. All the dishes were delicious. The wine range was extensive and we both had a perfect cocktail to start. The young waitress did her best but I was super impressed by the maître de and chef who supported her doing a good job. The staff were all attentive to the needs of all the tables. Parking, after commuting hours, was easy. We will be going back, quite soon I expect.

What a fabulous restaurant! Absolutely delicious food. The menu has lots of variety and the tapas style is a great way to experience and share the tempting delights not to mention the great wines on offer. Loved the atmosphere and the service was sensational. Have dined here a few times before and will return again and again.
This place is such a gem. The atmosphere and the wine list are fantastic, Service is always good and the food is amazing. Perfect spot for a date or to catch up with friends.

It’s a lovely experience at Kipling’s today! The meals are delicious and beautifully prepared. The staff is very friendly and welcoming; the venue is so nice and cosy. We will definitely be back again to try more dishes!

Love this new find - great food and vibe!
Staff are very friendly and efficient.
The only slight negative is a lack of vegetable dishes, though this was remedied by the chef cooking us an extra serve of chargrilled broccolini - excellent! 😊

name: Kokoe
address: 551 High St, Penrith NSW 2750, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: What can I say, this little cafe offers a range of Filipino meals and traditional cafe style cafes. The food has a home cooked feel, but could be a little more refined for the price, the pork dishes were nice, but the pasta was underwhelming. The coffee was nice

Great place for authentic Filipino dishes. Place was spacious and staff was friendly. Service is fast as well. Had the sisig. It was porky and crispy. Also had their Halo Halo. Is everything you want in a typical halo halo (missing the ube ice cream).

I had their congee twice, delivered and in restaurant.  Their congee is bursting with flavour and a good amount of ginger.

I've also had their crispy pork and garlic rice delivered in the past,  also very delicious and will order again.

Service in restaurant is lovely but for congee,  there was a bit of a long wait,  around 20 minutes.

I just had brunch here, and it was wonderful. My first impression was the setting. Everything is nicely presented and super clean. The staff are so kind and helpful, the food is tasty and so was the tropical drink. I'm so glad I have found a local Filipino cafe/restaurant. I will be coming back. Thanks Kokoe.
Filipino Cafe in Penrith serving delicious classics. We had late lunch here on a Sunday. Space is nice and welcoming.

name: Kuya's Kape
address: 836 Pittwater Rd, Dee Why NSW 2099, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Kuya Kape has an amazing coffee and and amazing Filipino food. Their customer service is great with all of them being so kind to every customer that walks through the door.
Filipino or not, this place has great cofffee and awesome food. Customer service is the best and the price is just right.
This place is genuinely amazing with really nice staff

name: La Crema Cusina Penrith
address: 12 The Crescent, Penrith NSW 2750, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
reviews: Great place, customer service, food, delish & coffee is one of our faves! Best go to cafes in the riff

Such a great find! I love the food here so delicious and healthy (lots of veggie options as well as can add meat protein for those that like it) as well as some treats if you want. My fav is the Buddha bowl. Easy parking. It’s my must go place when I’m in Penrith.

I have been looking for a good Filipino cafe (modernised with a twist) in Penrith area and finally came across this cafe. They have a wide variety of menu choices that are fusion and mouth watering. I used the app and even got 15% off. I tried their Eggplant Katsu Focaccia. I wanted to add the pork belly but the app was not allowing me to do so but the staff very kindly gave this as an upgrade for free. I also got ube matcha latte which has a good hint of ube. You can possibly add sugar to your liking as it is not sweet. Definitely, good food, service and experience and will be back with my family.

I had the pleasure of eating here today. I went there totally unaware that the place had changed owners.

Well, what can I say?, I had the most beautiful Arancini dish presented perfectly on the plate!

The staff are lovely, the atmosphere is warm and friendly and the food and the coffee are outstanding!

Will definitely be back!

It was nice to pop into this calming vibes cute little cafe to celebrate mothers day with Mum. Friendly staff, amazing service was quick with coffee and the meal was delicious pork belly bao bun was chefs kiss. I would definitely be back!

name: Leenez Telopea Take Away Shop
address: 10/7-16 Benaud Pl, Telopea NSW 2117, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Delicious authentic Filipino food with warm and friendly customer service!
Yum! Giniling so worth it and big portion. Unable to finish everything in one go😂
Have tried a few different options now. Would happily recommend  anything on the menu. Good prices and friendly staff

Pinoy burger is AMAZING!! I order it every week with an iced mocha. The rest of their food tastes so good, they have a wide range including homemade Filipino dishes. The family working there are so lovely and always have a smile on their face. Definitely recommend!!
This Takeaway is definitely worth visiting for breakfast lunch or dinner. The food is fresh and served with the biggest smiles from the warming staff. Your tastebuds wont be let down

name: Li'l Darlin Crows Nest
address: 23 Willoughby Rd, Crows Nest NSW 2065, Australia
description: A verified Filipino community in Sydney.
reviews: Me and my sister had the best time here for my birthday. I am coeliac and they accommodated for me in the best way possible. Kira and Vit were legendary and made my day feel very special. Would definitely recommend and go again.
Amazing bottomless brunch. Calamari was the best I’ve had in Australia. Service was beyond amazing. Great vibes and music. Value can’t be beaten. Highly recommend.

Was really pleased with my experience at the Surry Hills store, so decided to visit this one. The vibe is great! However, I was a bit disappointed that there was no mushroom sauce, it was so good at the Surry Hills store.

Also, my fish burger was a little too salty, and I found around four small bones in the fillet, which was surprising as I didn’t expect that in a fish burger. Hence the four stars.

Wow - way above expectations!!!
Andre was an amazing host, Hannah kept the cocktails flowing & Rie, the head chef, served the most scrumptious food. We really enjoyed our day & to top it off it was exceptional value for money. We will return soon!!

I walked in thinking this was just a normal bar-style dining spot, but I was pleasantly surprised. The food here is much better than expected, and it honestly impressed me. The crispy Peking duck pizza was the star—it tasted exactly like a legit Peking duck wrap but in pizza form. Such a clever and delicious idea. Another standout was the cheese pizza with honey—one of the few places in Sydney where I’ve found this, and it worked perfectly with the richness of the cheese. We also tried tapas on their Wednesday special for $12.50, which is such good value. The burrata with porcini mushrooms (highly recommended by the staff) was a great balance of freshness, oil, and a hint of spice. Everything paired nicely with the cocktails, which were just as enjoyable. Overall, a fantastic spot with different promotions throughout the week—definitely worth checking out.

name: Lola Cocina Spanish Restaurant Narrabeen
address: 1304 Pittwater Rd, Narrabeen NSW 2101, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Every dish we tried was absolutely delicious.
I was already so full that I couldn’t try more tapas and other menu items, which honestly makes me a little sad — I’ll definitely have to come back for them.

The sangria was the best I’ve ever had. Truly outstanding.
We’re visiting on holiday, but this is one of those places I’d happily come back to just to eat here again.

Not only was the food incredible, but the service was exceptional and perfectly matched the quality of the food.
The restaurant interior and music selection created such a great atmosphere — it’s been a long time since I’ve visited a place this memorable and delightful.
It genuinely made our holiday even happier.

We loved it so much that we even got takeaway to enjoy later!

If you’re visiting the area or live nearby, definitely come and try this place!

I visited during lunch time and had a wonderful experience. The restaurant is clean and comfortable. Our waiter, Mahez, was very friendly and helpful. I also really enjoyed the drink he made for us (as shown in the photo).

We ordered the seafood Paella and the lunch special Tapas plate — both were excellent! (See the promotion in the picture.)

We will definitely come back again! The place, the service from Mahez, the food, and the price are all 👍.

Fantastic Spanish restaurant and incredibly accommodating for gluten free.

Very tasty food (we used to live in Barcelona). Slightly over spiced on the paella but nothing some lemon juice didn’t solve. Perfect pan con tomate, croquetas and pastas bravas. Gambas cooked perfectly but needed a bit more seasoning. Still took home our leftovers. Yum.

Staff were wonderful. Very busy for only
3 staff members so a little wait on service but we didn’t book ahead so completely understandable.

Thanks and we will be back!

The service was lovely as Mahez welcomed us immediately and the chatted with us while we waited for our meal. He was wonderful with his recommendations, and we ended up ordering the Paella de Marisco, Vegetarian Tapas (Lunch Special), and Sangria. Everything was incredibly tasty, so I would certainly recommend checking this place out whenever you’re in the area. I’ve been to a couple of their venues, and I have to say, this one has the best and friendliest service.
Awesome lunch today! Food and service was excellent! The pork belly, wings and paella were the standouts! Booked under the firsttable 50% off food promotion so it was real value for money! 8/10

name: Mabuhey App - Online Filipino Groceries Food and Products
address: 710 George St, Haymarket NSW 2000, Australia
hours: {"mon": "Open 24 hours", "tue": "Open 24 hours", "wed": "Open 24 hours", "thu": "Open 24 hours", "fri": "Open 24 hours", "sat": "Open 24 hours", "sun": "Open 24 hours"}
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.

name: Macheon Korean Restaurant
address: 4/38 President Ave, Caringbah NSW 2229, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Been wanting to visit and try since I found out about this restaurant. Service was attentive and intimate as the restaurant was quite small and more formal than ones I visited. Food menu not a long list but sufficient for those who craves for Korean food and did well. We’re happy with our order and also took the dessert home. Worth a visit!

Small but mighty! Unassuming set back from the road, this restaurant delivers! The tteboki had great flavour, the japche noodles were chewy, and the spicy soup was rich and good level spicy. The Korean take on tiramisu was interesting but worth a try. I recommend booking. I will be returning!

As a kdrama loving Filipino, this place hit all the right spots! The food tasted like it came straight out of a kdrama sceen so good lol. I almost looked around for a dramatic plot twist. Cozy vibes, friendly staff, and you can really tell there’s heart in every dish. I’m definitely coming back and bringing friends with me next time.

Me and my family came here for the first time as we are from Canberra. The food was the best Korean food we've ever had. The lady who served us and the cook himself were very kind to us, and the atmosphere was very calming. We will definitely be coming back to this restaurant on our next visit to Cronulla👍

I appreciate good food. I appreciate Macheon. I feel like I got teleported back to the streets of Tanjong Pagar in Singapore!

The rice melts off the spoon, chicken batter succulent and crispy — full of flavour, and temperature perfect the moment it was served. Ingredients were high quality and felt soul-filling. Even the sides tasted super fresh.

Everything was amazing from scratch to end, service impeccable, staff were very friendly and constantly checked in to see how we were doing.

New favorite in the Shire!

name: Miguelitos Diner Campbeltown
address: 200 Gilchrist Dr, Campbelltown NSW 2560, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
When you're missing Jollibee and you want to fill that craving.
The prices for a 1 piece chicken meals are a bit high, but that makes the family meal worth it. The size of the pieces are roughly 1.5x the size of KFC. They're very moist and juicy. And the skin is exceptionally crispy. The chicken is seasoned well.
The gravy is very buttery and well seasoned.
You can miss this spot as it in the corner and the nameplate isn't facing a thoroughfare.
They also had taho and other Filipino style desserts.
Food is good but didn’t like the gravy. Could make the shop easy to find tho. Paid $107 for 3 meals and 2 halo halo , and this is in the food court. Bit expensive I reckon

Total rip off! The chicken and chips weren’t even nice and it’s so overpriced. I paid $20 for 2 x chicken drumsticks, a small serving of chips, and a can of drink 😧There are so many other nicer places close by so I would avoid this place.

If you’re missing the famous Chickenjoy and gravy from Jollibee, this place is definitely worth visiting. As a Filipino, sometimes we really crave those familiar flavors from home, and it’s nice to find something that can satisfy that craving.

The chicken was very decent, crispy, and juicy, and the gravy really reminded me of the classic taste that we love. It may not be exactly the same as Jollibee back in the Philippines, but it definitely hits the spot when you’re missing it. The meal was very satisfying and comforting.

It’s always a great feeling when you find a place that can bring back a little taste of home. Perfect for Filipinos who are craving Chickenjoy vibes, and even for anyone who just wants to enjoy some good fried chicken and gravy.

Overall, a very enjoyable experience and I would happily come back again whenever I’m craving those familiar Filipino fast-food flavors. Highly recommended! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
The chicken is nice and similar to Jollibee’s, but overall it’s very expensive, and a bit overpriced.

name: Mix Mix Co. Express
address: Macquarie St, Liverpool NSW 2170, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: My husband and I have our own favorites. I love the keso fruit salad — it’s creamy, refreshing, and not too sweet. My husband loves the Avo Smash, which is always fresh, flavorful, and satisfying. We also both love the Taho - warm, silky, and just the right amount of sweetness. Our kids love the soft serve in a cone ☺️☺️

The most unique and delicious selection of Filipino sweet treats. Always comes with the most amazing service every time we visit. Our personal favorite is the taho. Takes me back to my childhood every time. Creamy and silky tofu, soft pearls with just the right bite and the perfect amount of brown sugar syrup that ties everything together beautifully.

Been coming here on and off for the past few months on a Friday afternoon towards closing time after work. Their drinks/desserts are eye catching and really good. I have tried a few of the flavours but I think the mais con yelo is my favourite. The owner is friendly and have accommodated my reasonable requests to modify my drink (albeit its not busy). I will continue to come back again. Thank you!

Our family usually buy ourselves some Filipino sweet treats at Mix Mix Co after finishing our shopping at Parklea. The "Halo Halo" dessert is usually a go to for my husband and my in-laws (I personally dont like them as much as they are a bit too sweet for my taste). There are other lighter options (e.g. Sago) if you prefer.

The staff is always so helpful. The quality control is also high standard. One time, I ordered some peach pies and the staff ended up telling me that they didnt feel comfortable serving me the pies (they did give me an option to wait while they can cook some more pies). Honestly I wouldnt have been able to tell had they not told me.

If you have a sweet tooth and want the best tasting dessert in Sydney then Head Over to Mix Mix Co. Their treats are beautifully colourful and very tasty. If you have any questions just ask the very friendly ppl in the shop for clarification. They have a dessert for you for every climate. Hot, warm or Cold.

name: Mix N Match Bakehouse Doonside
address: 4 Hill End Rd, Doonside NSW 2767, Australia

reviews:
We ordered CUTE Cakes for my niece’s birthday! Big thanks to Jenny the baker for accommodating us and putting so much effort into our requested design. We love it and my niece can't stop eating it! Haha.

Special thanks as well to the lovely lady at the counter for recommending the bibingka and puto cheese, we’ve now found some Filipino pastries we absolutely love! Such wonderful Filipino girls. Definitely a must-try bakeshop in Western Sydney!

Contrary to what others have said, I had a pleasant experience with the staff this Monday, 13/10/25. Ate was friendly and attentive, she also offered other products. We had the cheese bread, ensaymada, spanish bread, and the empanada(could be warmer).

My husband and I visited Mix & Match today and were disappointed with the service. Even though the Christmas music created a festive atmosphere, the staff member at the counter did not smile, greet us, or acknowledge us. She seemed uninterested and distracted.
We purchased balut, lumpia sariwa, and mamon, but the lady who served us was rude and didn’t smile the entire time. This kind of attitude is not acceptable in customer service and really affects the store’s image.

Hi, this is my first time buying your puto queso, and I found this metal piece in one of them. I really love puto, but seeing this made me very concerned about safety it could be quite dangerous, especially for kids. Please be more careful when preparing your products.

I visited this Filipino shop a few days ago and had a wonderful time chatting with the lady at the counter while she explained the different breads, sweets, and cakes to me. I tried the turon and ube cake, they were delicious! I’ll definitely be coming back.

Their service has always been the best. They are very friendly, well mannered and very professional. Aside from that, the food has always been excellent! Made fresh as always. A budget friendly catering services here in Sydney. Very highly recommended.

What impressed us most was the professionalism and attentiveness of the staff. They were friendly, efficient, and always one step ahead, ensuring that everything ran smoothly. Our guests were consistently attended to, and the service was impeccable without being intrusive.
We can’t thank Okasyon Catering Services enough for turning our wedding into an extraordinary event. Their dedication to quality and service made our special day even more memorable. If you’re looking for catering that will elevate your celebration to the next level, look no further than Okasyon Catering Services. We wholeheartedly recommend them for any event

All our guests were impressed with the scrumptious selection of food Okasyon served on my daughter’s 1st. description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
𝗣𝗔𝗗𝗗𝗬𝗦 𝗙𝗢𝗢𝗗 𝗠𝗔𝗥𝗞𝗘𝗧𝗦 - 𝗔 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗦𝗧 𝗙𝗢𝗥 𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗦𝗘𝗡𝗦𝗘𝗦 𝗪𝗜𝗧𝗛 𝗩𝗜𝗡𝗧𝗔𝗚𝗘 & 𝗘𝗫𝗢𝗧𝗜𝗖 𝗥𝗜𝗗𝗘𝗦!

If you’re looking for an experience that hits all the right notes—food, culture, and some of the dopest cars and bikes you’ll ever see—Paddy’s Food Markets is where it’s at!

This place is pure vibes.

From the moment you step in, you’re hit with the mouthwatering aroma of international flavors. Food trucks line the market, serving up everything from juicy burgers to authentic street tacos, aromatic Asian delicacies, and rich, decadent desserts. Every bite was chef’s kiss!

And then, the real show-stoppers—the vintage and exotic cars and motorcycles. Imagine classic muscle cars, sleek supercars, and rare bikes from different eras, all gleaming under the lights. Whether you’re a car enthusiast or just love seeing a lineup of pure automotive art, this exhibition is next level.

But what truly makes Paddy’s special is the people. The energy here is electric—families, car lovers, foodies, and people from all cultures coming together to enjoy a night of great eats and even greater company. It’s a melting pot of flavors, sounds, and styles, making every visit an unforgettable experience.

If you haven’t been yet, do yourself a favor and check it out. Come hungry, bring your camera, and soak in the good vibes!

Love the night market vibe here at Paddy's Night Food Market. Very vibrant and alive with lots of food choices and events happening around.

We went on a Filipino food night event, which coincided with a car show which was awesome.

First off, it was easy to get to via M4. Then there are lots of parking above, with very organized traffic control as you enter the area.
Great car show every month.Good food,great vibe awesome cars

Love the paddy market here! Especially when there’s a food festival been held here. Couple of month ago it was philippine Food Festival,and just yesterday it was Malaysian Food festival.
The food there is always amazing with cars meet.
The line can be really long at ever food stalls but it was worth the wait!
It was great. There are a lot of choices for food. Kids jumping castle and live band. A car show as well. It was a good night out.

name: Pagasa Asian Food Imports
address: 32 Binney Rd, Kings Park NSW 2148, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews:
I was scammed. Be careful.

I called Susan the owner a month before to advise her of a remittance in October.  I  wanted to remit in instalment but she wanted lump sum. I called and texted the figure. She sent me a form and both information were the same amount. I deposited the money. Her quote was in writing.

She remitted money to me in instalment . After 11 days, she sent me an amount different from what she quoted and gave several strange excuses.

When I sent all the above evidence to job her memory, she became rude and told me to do what I need to do. The tone was very different now that she owes me money.
It was take it or leave it.

I called her a crook.
She owes me money and refuses to pay.

Everything here is documented.

If your a Filipino I highly recommend here for all your food needs from home.
Just the smell brings back memories of the mother land.
Great customer service

name: PanaDarna
address: 55 Rooty Hill Rd N, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
reviews:
What a fantastic find! 🌟 Visited PanaDarna located at the entrance of The Kamayan and had such a wonderful experience with the family during Mother’s Day. You can really feel the warmth of a Filipino family-run business, and everything we tried was fresh, comforting, and full of flavour.

I brought my family along and everyone really enjoyed the experience. My family enjoyed the iced lattes while I went for the iced chocolate, which was rich, refreshing, and delicious. Their famous buko pie absolutely lived up to the hype and is definitely a must-try.

It’s always special seeing Filipino-owned businesses bringing people together through culture, hospitality, and great food here in Western Sydney. Wishing the team at PanaDarna all the best as they continue to grow. Definitely looking forward to coming back again!

name: Parklea Markets
address: 601 Sunnyholt Rd, Glenwood NSW 2768, Australia
description: Buzzing market hall featuring over 100 fresh produce, clothing, fast food & assorted goods vendors.
reviews:
I visited this market today and found it quite good overall. The experience was great, with so many shops available under one roof. The wide range of fresh vegetables and fruits is definitely a big plus. Apart from that, you can purchase almost everything a household needs, which makes it very convenient.

If you live nearby, it’s a good place to visit. However, I did find the prices of fruits and vegetables a bit more expensive compared to some other places in Sydney. On the positive side, the kids’ zone is a bonus. The fish and meat shops are another advantage, and there are plenty of fresh juice shops—which I absolutely loved.

Open from Friday to Sunday. We spent a productive morning shopping for fresh fruits and veggies. The prices were really good. We also got some gardening stuff. The Filipino food at Gate 1 was delicious. Get the chicken bbq and siopao.

Located at 601 Sunnyholt Road, Glenwood, New South Wales, Australia 🇦🇺
This is a pretty good market open Friday, Saturday and Sunday from 8.30am until 4.30pm. It has a fantastic nursery section selling all kinds of plants and outdoor decorations. There’s also fresh produce ie fruit and vegetables along with fresh seafood 🦞 and meat 🥩 products. I don’t, however, think the prices are any cheaper than if you were going to a supermarket. There’s plenty of clothing available, but don’t expect anything but basic everyday things, nothing really designer. There is always Australian 🇦🇺 souvenirs and toys, beauty products, sweets 🍭 and nuts, luggage and bags, jewellery, novelty items and more. There’s plenty of parking 🅿️ and good toilet facilities. There is also ATM 🏧 facilities. Worth a visit. 👍🙂

I visited Parklea Market for grocery shopping, and it’s truly a wide and well-organized market. You can find everything under one roof, which makes shopping very convenient. The market is open on Friday, Saturday, and Sunday, and if you go on Sunday, you can grab some really good deals on almost everything. A great spot to explore for groceries and more!
Was my first time visit and loved the green vegetables. I found Fiji apples. Best place to buy veggies from. Had breakfast at the dosa place and loved it. Highly recommend

name: Philippine Airlines
address: Level 4/49-51 York St, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia
description: A verified Filipino community in Sydney.
reviews: I have a connecting return flight from Davao to Manila to Brisbane. I called the pal customer service to advice that I will forfeit my local flight and said I will start my journey from Manila to Brisbane 3 days before my flight date. The guy I was talking to said there is no problem with that and I can just check in at Manila international airport. I did ask him if there anything I  have to do and he said no and that I am all set. When I got to Manila international airport I was told that they could not check me in because I did not follow my flight sequence and I am in danger of losing my flight as a consequence unless I have to pay the reissuance fee of $150 so I can fly home with my wife.I did explain that I have done all my diligence to call and inform the Airlines about my plan and I was promise of no dramas. I submitted a complaint to the complaint department and all they offer me is sorry for their mistake that cost me money . After flying with them for so many years this is what I've got. So disappointing

I am about to travel to Manila from Sydney with PAL with my family of 6. Yesterday they changed the aircraft type and seating layout. I spent a lot of time organising the seats over 2 booking to ensure we had 5 seats in a row and none of my children were next to a stranger. Also paid extra for this. When they reallocated the seats, the system allocated my 9 year old to a non existent seat. So now he has no seat and there is a stranger sitting between my partner and my 6 year old son.

Over 8 hours of chatting online as you cannot call them. All of the solutions offered were ridiculous. Been escalated several times. They can’t move the random person to another seat without their agreement, but had no problem moving my children without mine. Rather than relaxing getting ready for a holiday, I am up all night stressing about this.

We got quite a good deal fly from Taipei to Sydney via Manila. The business class both for short and long haul are comfortable and good service. The seat for short haul has a foot rest which is my favorite. The long haul has lay flat seat (with massage function), excellent space, touch screen TV, plenty of entertainments. The teams deliver wonderful service. They are very thoughtful, prepare a Lunar New Year Red Pocket with a chocolate while serving the lunch. If there was anything I would ask for more, probably is the food-they are good and could be better. One more thing, they are not affiliated with any of the three major airline alliances currently. So you wouldn’t be able to accumulate your mileage except their own Mabuhay Miles frequent flyer program.

A Disappointing and Irresponsible Experience

I deeply regret choosing Philippine Airlines, as my experience with them has been nothing short of frustrating and disappointing. They demonstrated an appalling level of irresponsibility and lack of accountability when it came to handling our luggage, and their customer service was completely ineffective.

During our trip, they damaged and lost parts of my wife's walking frame, an essential mobility aid. I immediately reported the issue and provided all necessary documentation, including tickets and photos of the damaged frame. Despite this, Philippine Airlines failed to follow up or take any meaningful action.

Over the past several months, I have contacted them more than 12 times via phone, email, and social media—each time being reassured that I would receive a follow-up call. That call never came. Instead, they repeatedly ignored our case, gave false promises, and played dumb whenever I followed up.

This airline has proven itself to be extremely negligent in handling customer complaints and utterly disinterested in resolving issues that directly impact their passengers. A responsible airline should acknowledge its mistakes and compensate customers for damages they have caused. Philippine Airlines did neither.

In the end, they lost us as customers, and I strongly advise others to avoid flying with them. If they treat a case involving a medical necessity with such disregard, I can only imagine how they handle other issues. Fly with an airline that values its passengers—Philippine Airlines is not one of them

I’m surprised with the quality, punctuality and kindness from all staff.
I got a very good deal, but wasn’t expecting too much. The airplanes are amazing and very comfortable. I will definitely flight with Philippine Airlines again 💙

name: Pinoy Stop Ingleburn
address: 44 Carlisle St, Ingleburn NSW 2565, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: All you need for filipino stores. Many varieties and brands to choose from. But aisle spaces are too narrow to fit two-way traffic. Staff are friendly and kind.

A good filo grocery in the south west where there is a lot of options specially when one is looking for bangus, longganisa, hot dogs etc. and price is affordable for a filo grocery that is 40+km away from city centre.
There for the lunch menu. Plenty of customers on a Saturday so it must be good. We weren't disappointed, good food at a great price. Will be back again for sure
Good variety of stock which we could only usually get from the Blacktown area before, so glad we can it locally now

Good range of products.
Easy to move around the shop.
Pricing can be abit more bigger.
Neat and tidy and checkout lady friendly.

name: Pinoy ‘To Atbp Cafe
address: 109 Oxford St, Bondi Junction NSW 2022, Australia
description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
reviews:
A hidden gem serving up wonderful Filipino food, right in Bondi Junction! This time we tried Lechon Kawali (crispy and tender pork belly) as well as sizzling Sisig (pork with chicken liver) with a side of garlic rice. All were delicious. Service also friendly, and food appeared on our table within minutes.

Advice for the undecided: Walk in past the unassuming storefront, through to the end and give these guys a go. Their menu is in Filipino, so expect to Google, but there is such a wide range on offer, suited to anyone from the faint to brave hearted.

Bad service! Asked for a menu and couldn’t provide me one, I had to open Uber Eats to see their menu and when I was ready to order, been told to wait until they are finished cooking the other patron’s food. Ambiance is none existent, place is hot and humid, not enough seats to accommodate more than 10. Food may be good but, couldn’t say/judge if they can’t even provide service. Pity!

This is one the best filo restaurants I have been to in Sydney! Their food is delicious, I have been back multiple times with my partner. We have tried quite a few filo restaurants around Sydney and this is by far the best one! Definitely try the sisig and Ginataang Kalabasa

Extremely bad service. Waited almost an hour for food to arrive (3 dishes), and even then, it was not worth the wait. The store at the front has a good variety of Asian grocery items, but the service, like the cafe, is poor.
What a great little cafe. Very casual in the back of a Filipino supermarket. Extremely tasty, affordable, authentic food. What a treat! Great for something quick and delish.

name: Potato Station Rooty Hill
address: 52 Rooty Hill Rd N, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: I bought a piece of bbq for my son then when we went home I saw the bbq it was toasted/ burned, so hard to bite 😔

Firstly, the name doesn't fit to the type of main food that this place is offering.  Though it's a knock-off of the much-known brand Potato Corner,  their potato fries offering is hardly the reason why people come to this place.  People come to eat the silogs!

Last Sunday, me and wife went to Rooty Hill to have brunch.  We usually go to Kamayan to have breakfast for a legit lutong bahay dish but we decided to check out the place called Potato Station to check out their offerings. Since they have silogs, we decided to go in and dine for a change.

I ordered Longsilog and my wife ordered Lekasilog. My order was served first and minutes later, my wife's.  They served Hotsilog instead.  My wife turned it down as she ordered a Lekasilog.  On the other table, an Aussie guy ordered Corsilog (corned beef) but was served a bistek. Instead of apologising, the owner joked around to the guy to say it nicely in tagalog accent so he will be clearly understood.  For me that's rude and not welcoming.

On my wife's case, instead of apologising, she told us that the kitchen staff is half-asleep! You don't tell your customer that. Let alone trying to joke around to your customers that you just met.  Take your business seriously as well as your customers. If you want to know what customer service really is, go to Takam in Darlinghurst or Lencia's in Castle Hill.
The food is great tasting and the atmosphere along with the staff make it a great place to eat in or takeaway.

What a lucky and wonderful discovery here in Rooty Hill! 🌟 Visited the newly opened Potato Station with the family during Mother’s Day and honestly came away very impressed. From the moment we entered, there was such a warm and welcoming atmosphere that made the experience feel both comfortable and memorable.

The space itself is beautifully set up with a modern yet authentic feel. Plenty of natural sunlight comes through the venue, the ventilation is excellent, and the overall ambience feels cosy while still being fresh and contemporary. It’s the kind of place where you can comfortably enjoy a meal, catch up with family and friends, spend time working quietly, or even host a larger gathering. The venue feels very versatile and community-oriented, with seating arrangements that work well for small groups while also having the capacity for bigger events and celebrations.

Accessibility is also excellent, especially being located right next to a major car park which makes visiting very convenient.

Food and drinks were fantastic. We tried the loaded cheesy bacon fries which were generous, flavourful, and perfect for sharing. I also had the iced white chocolate while another family member enjoyed the iced matcha, both of which were refreshing and well made. What stood out was the variety across the menu, with breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks, breads, and drinks all available, making it a place you could easily return to multiple times and still have something new to try.

You can really feel the heart behind the business too. It’s always inspiring seeing Filipino-owned and woman-led businesses creating welcoming community spaces here in Western Sydney. The hospitality shown throughout the visit was genuinely appreciated.

I give Potato Station my highest recommendation and can absolutely see why it’s quickly becoming one of the hottest new spots across Rooty Hill, the Blacktown LGA, and broader Western Sydney. Looking forward to coming back again and wishing the team all the very best for the upcoming grand launch on 23 May 2026!

We went to habe a breakfast with my 2 driends and son today the staff was not that aporachable and seem not  to be aporachable my friend ordered tapa siloh the other  friend bangsilong my son beef steak and i ordered longsilong my 2 friends oder came on time mine and my son took forevwr and my 2 driends was almost finish having there breakfast and my order  and my son hasnt came yet and when it was time the stafg gici g me a difderent one that i did not oder will not reccommed vwry slow servixe ans dont have staff who knows wjat there doing not even busy food was ok bit was not happy of the experience we had with this restaurant
